versions.reason: 3.13 ecosystem compatibility not yet validated across portfolio. 3.12 confirmed stable across all apps.
Use slim, not alpine. Alpine lacks glibc and causes issues with compiled dependencies like asyncpg and psycopg2.
Latest stable 0.115.x. Requires pydantic v2. Do not use older versions — missing async lifespan support.
Always install uvicorn[standard] to include websocket and http2 extras. Latest stable.
SQLAlchemy 2.1.x introduces async session breaking changes not yet validated in portfolio. Always use 2.0-style async (AsyncSession, async_sessionmaker). Do NOT use legacy 1.x patterns.
Pairs with SQLAlchemy 2.0.x. Run migrations at container startup (not build time). Use async env.py pattern.
Async PostgreSQL driver. Required for FastAPI + SQLAlchemy async pattern. Use postgresql+asyncpg:// in DATABASE_URL.
Sync PostgreSQL driver. Use only when async is not applicable. Prefer asyncpg for all FastAPI apps.
Pydantic v2 only. All models use v2 syntax (model_config = ConfigDict(...), not class Config). FastAPI 0.115.x requires v2. Treat v1-to-v2 as a full breaking migration.
Settings management from env vars and .env files. Use BaseSettings with ConfigDict(env_file='.env').
Load .env for local development. pydantic-settings handles this automatically when configured.
Async HTTP client. Used as FastAPI test client (TestClient replacement for async) and for outbound API calls.
Redis Queue for background workers (Flavor C apps). Pairs with redis-py 5.x.
Python Redis client. Import as 'redis'. Pairs with rq 1.16.x.
Test runner. Target 80% coverage minimum. Pairs with pytest-asyncio for async test support.
Async test support for FastAPI + SQLAlchemy async apps. Use asyncio_mode='auto' in pytest.ini.
FastMCP framework for building MCP servers in Python. Mounts as ASGI app on FastAPI. Use streamable HTTP transport.
PostgreSQL 16 Alpine for Docker compose services. Never use :latest. For Coolify managed DB, select PG 16 explicitly.
Redis 7 Alpine. Always configure appendonly yes for queue durability. Redis is cache/queue only — not primary datastore.
Node 20 Alpine for Next.js apps. Never source-build Next.js on VPS — always pre-build via GitHub Actions and push to GHCR.
Next.js 14 App Router. Never source-build on the VPS — CPU spikes stall Coolify deploys. Pre-build via GitHub Actions.
Prisma 5.x ORM for Next.js apps. Pairs with node 20 and nextjs 14.
TypeScript 5.x for all Node projects.
React 18.x. Pairs with vite 5. Do not use Create React App — Vite only.
Vite 5 for React frontends. No Create React App.
Tailwind CSS 3.x. Tailwind 4 not yet validated in portfolio.
TanStack Query v5 for data fetching in React apps.
Recharts 2.x for charting in React apps.

rules.rule: Never source-build Next.js or any TypeScript/Node app on the VPS via Coolify.
rules.reason: Host CPU cannot handle npm build. Causes CPU spikes and stalled Coolify deploys. Always pre-build via GitHub Actions and push image to GHCR.

rules.rule: Never commit secrets, .env files, or API keys to git.
rules.reason: Security. All secrets go in BWS (Bitwarden Secrets Manager). Coolify injects them as environment variables at deploy time.

rules.rule: Never use the :latest tag for any Docker image (postgres, redis, node, python, etc.).
rules.reason: Unpredictable upgrades break reproducibility and can introduce breaking changes silently.

rules.rule: Disable Coolify scheduled jobs for Flavor C apps that have an in-stack scheduler sidecar.
rules.reason: Coolify cron running alongside the scheduler sidecar causes duplicated backups, health probes, and restore drills.

rules.rule: Coolify webhook redeploy trigger must fire after the GHCR image push completes, not before.
rules.reason: Early trigger causes Coolify to pull the previous stale image instead of the newly built one.

rules.rule: Never run Alembic migrations at Docker build time (in Dockerfile RUN steps).
rules.reason: No database connection exists at build time. Migrations must run at container startup in the CMD entrypoint.

rules.rule: Flavor A apps (SQLite) require a manually configured Coolify scheduled backup job.
rules.reason: Coolify's built-in auto-backup only works for its managed Postgres resources. SQLite backup requires a Coolify cron job calling backup.sh.

rules.rule: Do not use Redis as a primary datastore.
rules.reason: Redis is queue and cache only. All durable data must live in Postgres. Redis data loss on restart must be acceptable.

rules.rule: Use python:3.12-slim, not python:3.12-alpine, as the Python base image.
rules.reason: Alpine lacks glibc. Compiled Python dependencies (asyncpg, psycopg2, etc.) fail or require complex workarounds on Alpine.

rules.rule: Google OAuth redirect URIs must match exactly and must be registered per environment (production and preview separately).
rules.reason: A mismatch causes silent OAuth failure. Preview deployments need their own callback URI registered in Google Cloud Console.

rules.rule: Pin SHA or version tags for all third-party GitHub Actions steps.
rules.reason: Floating tags (e.g. actions/checkout@main) can be updated by the action author and introduce unexpected behavior.

rules.rule: Coolify health checks must target 127.0.0.1, not the public domain.
rules.reason: Coolify probes the internal container network. Using the public domain adds unnecessary DNS round-trips and can fail if DNS hasn't propagated.

rules.rule: If an app uses Basic Auth, exempt the health check path (/api/health) from authentication.
rules.reason: Coolify health probes do not send credentials. A protected health endpoint will always return 401 and Coolify will mark the app as unhealthy.

rules.rule: Query Infra Brain before pinning any dependency version or making any infrastructure decision.
rules.reason: Infra Brain is the source of truth. Do not infer standards from existing app code — that code may predate the standard.

rules.rule: Write back to Infra Brain when a new version is validated or a new lesson is learned.
rules.reason: The registry only stays current if agents and humans update it. A stale registry is worse than no registry.

combos.name: python-fastapi-stack
combos.description: Full Python FastAPI stack for Flavor B and C apps. Use this for any new Python/FastAPI project.

combos.name: python-fastapi-rq-stack
combos.description: Additional packages for Flavor C apps (workers + queue). Layer on top of python-fastapi-stack.

combos.name: nextjs-prisma-stack
combos.description: Full Next.js 14 App Router stack with Prisma ORM. For Flavor B Node apps.

combos.name: react-vite-stack
combos.description: React 18 + Vite 5 frontend stack. For standalone SPAs or FastAPI frontend.

lessons.title: Next.js source builds on VPS stall Coolify deploys
lessons.content: Attempting to build a Next.js or TypeScript app directly on the VPS via a Coolify source build causes CPU spikes that stall the entire Coolify deployment queue. The host VPS does not have enough CPU headroom for npm/next build operations. Solution: Always pre-build via GitHub Actions and push the image to GHCR. Coolify should only pull the pre-built image, never build it. This is now a BLOCK rule for all Node/TypeScript apps.

lessons.title: Google OAuth PKCE code verifier must persist across the redirect
lessons.content: FastAPI's OAuth flow for Google Calendar requires the PKCE code_verifier to persist between /admin/google/authorize and /admin/google/callback. If the verifier is stored only in memory or lost between requests (e.g. in a stateless handler), the callback will fail silently. Solution: Store the code_verifier in the session or database at the authorize step and retrieve it in the callback. Fixed in commit ea88d4d.

lessons.title: Google OAuth redirect URIs must match exactly per environment
lessons.content: Google Cloud Console validates OAuth redirect URIs exactly, including the subdomain. Production and preview deployments need separate callback URIs registered. Production: https://booking.devonwatkins.com/admin/google/callback. Preview: https://preview.booking.devonwatkins.com/admin/google/callback. Using the wrong URI causes a Google OAuth error with no useful debug information. Set GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI in BWS per environment.

lessons.title: Coolify scheduled jobs duplicate in-stack scheduler sidecar
lessons.content: Contact Hub runs a scheduler_sidecar.py as an in-stack Docker service that handles all periodic operations (health checks, backups, restore drills). If Coolify scheduled jobs are also configured for the same app, operations run twice — causing double backups, duplicate health probes, and potentially conflicting restore drills. Rule: Disable all Coolify scheduled jobs for Flavor C apps. The sidecar is the single scheduler.

lessons.title: Health check endpoint must be exempt from Basic Auth
lessons.content: Contact Hub uses Basic Auth to protect the API. Coolify's internal health check probe does not send credentials, so if /api/health is protected by Basic Auth, every health check returns 401 and Coolify marks the container as unhealthy. Solution: Configure BASIC_AUTH_EXEMPT_PATHS to include /api/health (and any other paths Coolify needs to probe). This is now documented in the infra-standards skill.

lessons.title: Google Calendar all-day events must be handled separately from timed events
lessons.content: Google Calendar's events.list() API returns all-day events with date fields (not dateTime fields). When checking calendar windows for booking availability, all-day events must be detected and handled explicitly. Offset-aware dateTime timestamps from Google must be normalized to UTC before local timezone conversion, otherwise calendar windows shift incorrectly. Fixed in the March 2026 Google reliability fixes (commit ea88d4d).
